steps.title: Look for matching slopes
steps.content: An equation has no solution when the two sides have the same $x$-coefficient but different constants. That creates parallel, distinct lines.

steps.title: Find the contradiction
steps.content:
The equation
$$-13x-\frac{1}{9}=-13x+\frac{1}{9}$$
has the same $x$-term on both sides. Subtracting $-13x$ leaves
$$-\frac{1}{9}=\frac{1}{9}$$
which is impossible.

steps.title: Choose the no-solution equation
steps.content: The impossible statement means that equation has no solution. The answer is B.
